testcase.name: test_exceptions

failure.message: AssertionError: "name 'res_df' is not defined" != 'Please check that your model is fitted, and accepts X as input.' - name 'res_df' is not defined + Please check that your model is fitted, and accepts X as input.

testcase.failure:
self = <tests.test_1D_ale.Test1DContinuous testMethod=test_exceptions>
def test_exceptions(self): # dataset should be compatible with the model with self.assertRaises(Exception) as mod_ex_2: aleplot_1D_continuous(self.X, self.model, "x1") mod_ex_msg = "Please check that your model is fitted, and accepts X as input." > self.assertEqual(mod_ex_2.exception.args[0], mod_ex_msg) E AssertionError: "name 'res_df' is not defined" != 'Please check that your model is fitted, and accepts X as input.' E - name 'res_df' is not defined E + Please check that your model is fitted, and accepts X as input.

testcase.name: test_exceptions

failure.message: AssertionError: "name 'res_df' is not defined" != 'There seems to be a problem when predict[370 chars] ' - name 'res_df' is not defined + There seems to be a problem when predicting with the model. Please check the following: - Your model is fitted. - The list of predictors contains the names of all the features used for training the model. - The encoding function takes the raw feature and returns the right columns encoding it, including the case of a missing category.

testcase.failure:
self = <tests.test_1D_ale.Test1Ddiscrete testMethod=test_exceptions>
def test_exceptions(self): mod_not_fit = RandomForestRegressor() # dataset should be compatible with the model with self.assertRaises(Exception) as mod_ex_1: aleplot_1D_categorical( X=self.X, model=self.model.predict, feature="x5", predictors=self.X_cleaned.columns, encode_fun=onehot_encode_custom, ) with self.assertRaises(Exception) as mod_ex_2: aleplot_1D_categorical( X=self.X, model=self.model, feature="x5", predictors=self.X.columns, encode_fun=onehot_encode_custom, ) with self.assertRaises(Exception) as mod_ex_3: aleplot_1D_categorical( X=self.X, model=self.model, feature="x5", predictors=self.X_cleaned.columns, encode_fun=onehot_encode, ) mod_ex_msg = ( """There seems to be a problem when predicting with the model. Please check the following: - Your model is fitted. - The list of predictors contains the names of all the features""" """ used for training the model. - The encoding function takes the raw feature and returns the""" """ right columns encoding it, including the case of a missing category. """ ) > self.assertEqual(mod_ex_1.exception.args[0], mod_ex_msg) E AssertionError: "name 'res_df' is not defined" != 'There seems to be a problem when predict[370 chars] ' E - name 'res_df' is not defined E + There seems to be a problem when predicting with the model. E Please check the following: E - Your model is fitted. E - The list of predictors contains the names of all the features used for training the model. E - The encoding function takes the raw feature and returns the right columns encoding it, including the case of a missing category.
tests/test_1D_ale.py:398: AssertionError
